claim: Roughly one in twenty UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues.

result.claim: Roughly one in twenty UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues.
result.reasoning: The knowledge base does not contain any direct evidence to support or refute the claim that roughly one in twenty UK citizens harbor variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in lymphoid tissues. Initial searches for the specific claim and related concepts like "prevalence of variant Creutzfeldt-Jakob disease prions in UK lymphoid tissues" yielded no relevant results. The retrieved propositions and chunks were primarily about genetic mutations (BRCA1, BRCA2, CHEK2) and other unrelated medical conditions, indicating a lack of information on vCJD prevalence in the UK within the available data.

evidence.text: The international sample comprised 11,900 carriers of BRCA2 mutations.
evidence.source: Association of type and location of BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations with risk of breast and ovarian cancer.

evidence.text: The CHEK2*1100delC allele has a high prevalence in the Netherlands.
evidence.source: Genomic profiling of CHEK2*1100delC-mutated breast carcinomas

evidence.text: An analysis of sera from 78 healthy HIV-1-infected donors demonstrated that 27% contained MPER-specific antibodies.
evidence.source: Broad and potent neutralization of HIV-1 by a gp41-specific human antibody

evidence.text: The prevalence of the CHEK2 1100delC allele was determined in index cases from 300 Australian multiple-case breast cancer families.
evidence.source: Low frequency of CHEK2 1100delC allele in Australian multiple-case breast cancer families: functional analysis in heterozygous individuals

evidence.text: Less than 20% of Pakistani women with early-onset or familial breast/ovarian cancer harbor germ line mutations in BRCA1, BRCA2 and TP53.
evidence.source: Constitutional CHEK2mutations are infrequent in early-onset and familial breast/ovarian cancer patients from Pakistan

evidence.text: The variant p. I160M was identified in 1/90 (1.1%) Indian controls.
evidence.source: Low Prevalence of CHEK2 Gene Mutations in Multiethnic Cohorts of Breast Cancer Patients in Malaysia

evidence.text: The 1100delC mutation occurred in 0.4% of controls in the Swedish population.

evidence.text: The 1100delC mutation occurred in 0.7% of cases in the Swedish population.

evidence.text: Four common mutations in the CHEK2 gene have been identified in the Polish population: 1100delC, IVS2+1G>A, del5395 and I157T.
evidence.source: Can selenium be a modifier of cancer risk in CHEK2 mutation carriers?

evidence.text: The international sample comprised 19,581 carriers of BRCA1 mutations.

evidence.text: The prevalence of Systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) is approximately 1 in 1000.
